bullets.content: Don't use mutable default arguments in Python (e.g., def foo(items=[]))
Always validate input at API boundaries before processing
Check for null/undefined before accessing nested object properties
Write tests before fixing bugs to prevent regression
Use environment variables for configuration, not hardcoded values
Log errors with context (request ID, user ID, operation) for debugging
Use try/finally or context managers to ensure resource cleanup
Prefer explicit imports over wildcard imports for clarity
Always read reference/world_book_index.md and reference/audit_report.md before writing any module involving numbers/geography/qualities. Never invent data.
After modifying types.ts (adding fields to interfaces), immediately run typecheck to find all test files that need updating
MapTopology/TopologyEdge in types.ts were dead code with zero references - deleting them is safe. Always grep for references before deleting types.
World book data source: reference/world_book_index.md maps all 605 entries. Key sections: tier values (#417617), geography (#580370 topology), quality names (#265160), combat protocol (#837805)
Default era name is fuxing-era, not glorious-era. Affection range is -100 to +100, not -50 to +100. Quality names: putong/youliang/xīyou/shishi/chuanshuo/shenhua/weiyi

AVOID: ADR-11 boundary: never assume a subsystem is pure-Code without asking. Crafting needs AI for creative effects (affix naming, descriptions), Code only does DC/check/settlement math. Clarify division BEFORE implementing.
AVOID: When adding quality-to-tier mappings, cross-reference tier-constants.ts qualityCap. 史诗 is first producible at T3 (not T4). mismatch propagated to 8 test failures.
AVOID: StatePatch uses 'op' not 'type' field. grep the target type definition before building patches, don't guess field names.
AVOID: BuildCraftDescription checked rating before prep-failure, causing resource-blocked crafts to say '失败' instead of '终止'. Order: prep-failure → 大失败 → 失败 → success.

After major type changes, run tsc --noEmit first → collect ALL failures → fix in batch. Went from 12 test failures to 0 in one iteration by fixing root causes (QUALITY_TIER_REFERENCE + production bonus DC) rather than patching individual tests.
Windows: use python.exe directly in PowerShell (not python3 or full path). This resolves to Python 3.10 at E:/Python310/python.exe.

When the user says 'plan is too complex, just give me bounds and an API', LISTEN. Affection system went from 6 functions (calcAffectionChange/getAffectionTier/etc.) to 6 simple methods (get/set/add/batch/label/simpleLabel). Code does clamping, AI does judgment.

AVOID: When writing integration scenario tests, always check actual function signatures first (grep '^export function' in source), not what you remember. combat-resolver exports resolveAttack not executeAttack; combat-turn exports rollInitiative not createCombatUnitTurn.

Integration scenario tests are worth the effort: 44 tests covering full combat flow (encounter→cluster→AoE→morale→execution→settlement) caught interface mismatches and validated ALL subsystems work together. Write them after completing a subsystem group.

AVOID: Don't put Prompt engineering before frontend. User explicitly said context visibility & prompt tuning goes AFTER UI because you need a running system to iterate on prompt quality. Plan order: backend → frontend → prompt tuning → integration.

AVOID: AppModal Escape key handler on div @keydown never fires without focus. Use document.addEventListener('keydown', handler) in onMounted + removeEventListener in onUnmounted. Always add a visible close button as fallback.

AVOID: Vue template expressions cannot use IIFE arrows or complex inline logic like (()=>{...})(). Use computed properties instead — define const activePreset = computed(() => presets.value.find(...)) in <script> and reference it in template.

AVOID: Declaring variables (showSaveModal) in multiple places when merging code blocks. When editing script setup, grep for existing declarations before adding new ones — vite build catches this but tsc --noEmit doesn't always.

CSS multi-theme system: use <html data-theme="xxx"> + CSS :root/[data-theme] selectors for all color/typography tokens. Use color-mix() for dynamic transparency (hover/focus/selected states). Store theme preference in localStorage. Each theme defines ~40 CSS custom properties in one file.

SillyTavern preset JSON structure: top-level has temperature/frequency_penalty/presence_penalty/top_p/openai_max_tokens etc. The 'prompts' array contains sub-prompt objects with: name, enabled (bool), role (system/user/assistant), content (string), identifier (UUID), injection_position/depth/order, marker (bool), forbid_overrides (bool), system_prompt (bool). Imported files should use filename as preset name.

When adding Vue 3 frontend to a tsc-only TypeScript project: keep tsc for engine compilation, use vite build for UI. Add separate build:engine script. Use path aliases (@engine) in both vite.config.ts and tsconfig.json paths. Add src/**/*.vue to tsconfig include. Create src/env.d.ts for .vue module declarations.
